source_or_gap: Cross-project synthesis of CCA, MoECOT Manifest, BeastBrain, BugBrain, Corben's Trainer, and Corben's Best Model Possible, treating them as one local architecture lineage and mapping shared mechanisms, contradictions, evidence limits, and exact ASI Stack insertions.
chapter_action_policy: Treat the six projects as one non-independent local implementation lineage and execute docs/historical_project_incorporation_roadmap.md existing-chapter first. Prioritize cross-cutting additions to the existing evidence, benchmark, artifact, architecture, compiler, routing, authority, memory, resource, readiness, and improvement chapters. Keep a durable-semantic-memory chapter boundary deferred until independent primary literature and overlap review are complete.
merge_or_split_policy: Merge the name-to-effect, causal capability chain, evidence cell, material transition, authority tuple, revocation closure, capacity ladder, router ladder, negative-knowledge frontier, semantic-depth, and honest-missingness laws into existing owners. Split out durable semantic memory only after independent literature confirms a unique thesis and chapter boundary.
boundary_decision_rationale: The projects form an evolutionary architecture lineage. Repeated mechanisms are not independent support and should be deduplicated into shared laws. Durable semantic memory remains the one substantial unowned substrate, but local historical projects alone are insufficient to add the chapter.
external_literature_need: knowledge graphs, temporal/event-sourced knowledge, graph-RAG, and hybrid vector/graph retrieval
learned graph navigation, agent long-term memory, consolidation, forgetting, and memory evaluation
causal provenance from training state to generated outputs and actions
measurement theory, metric provenance, benchmark validity, and missing-data semantics
transactional state machines, event sourcing, crash consistency, and transitive invalidation
capability security, least authority, revocation, and sandbox enforcement
adaptive computation, recurrent parameter sharing, learned routing, and interference
formal refinement, semantic preservation, proof abstraction, and runtime coupling
proof_or_test_backlog: validate a machine-readable evidence-cell schema across all six project case families
trace a model checkpoint causally through runtime decoding to a returned response and reject disconnected paths
mutate or quarantine an upstream artifact and prove transitive invalidation of evidence, claims, promotion, readiness, and reports
restart after every named durable transition and verify the material postcondition
enforce a unified authority tuple across approval, budget, execution, trace, replay, and revocation
test semantic-capacity claims across addressable, resident, active, populated, trainable, updated, reachable, and useful states
evaluate routers through the full specialist-existence to autonomous-outcome ladder
preserve and query rejected architecture families and retry ceilings as negative knowledge
attach semantic-depth and independence records to formal and tribunal evidence
prove missing evidence resolves to unknown/not-attempted/not-applicable rather than passed or real
evidence_transition_precondition: shared local lineage deduplicated and not counted as independent replication
claim-specific origin and implementation mapping complete
independent primary literature reviewed for generalized claims
external or independent evaluators used for empirical capability statements
accepted transition record required for any support-state change
required_before_drafting: execute Wave 0 source binding and terminology normalization
perform file/symbol provenance tracing where an origin statement matters
ingest durable semantic memory and evidence-provenance literature
preserve exact non-claims and avoid project-name promotional framing
redact private paths, raw artifacts, weights, corpora, credentials, and unpublished cases
promotion_blockers: the projects share authorship, terminology, code lineage, and architecture and are not independent supports
most full workspaces and target deployments were not reproduced
capability, external benchmark, speech, memory, routing, security, and safety results remain unverified
several retained artifacts are incomplete, quarantined, simulated, placeholder, declared, or non-relocatable
durable semantic memory lacks reviewed independent primary literature
new intake items may still be extracting
source-publication permission is not recorded for raw project material
residuals: symbol-level inheritance across DKL/Portia, compiler, context, router, tribunal, and governance code
exact set of newly extracted projects after final intake rescan
chapter-by-chapter passage drafting and claim/evidence acceptance
independent validation of the evidence-cell and durable-memory boundaries
complete preservation map for artifact corpora altered by cleanup
next_action: Execute the first six incorporation packets in order: Evidence States, Benchmark Ratchets, Artifact Graphs, Integrated Reference Architecture, System Boundaries, and Cognitive Compilation; then reassess the durable-memory ownership boundary after Waves 1 and 2.
non_claims: does not count repeated local concepts as independent evidence
does not promote any model, benchmark, architecture, security, memory, routing, speech, readiness, AGI, or ASI claim
does not add the deferred durable-semantic-memory chapter
does not change chapter order or support states
does not publish private project content
